steps.expected: Trait with 7 methods
steps.observed: Trait defined at lines 22-42 with all 7 methods: init_distributor, init_per_core(u32), acknowledge() -> Option<u32>, end_of_interrupt(u32), enable_irq(u32), disable_irq(u32), send_ipi(u32)

steps.expected: impl InterruptController for GicV3 with real code
steps.observed: All 7 methods implemented with real code (MMIO writes, inline asm for ICC registers, GICR configuration). No todo!() or unimplemented!() found.

steps.expected: No matches
steps.observed: No matches in .rs files. Only matches in DESIGN.md (documentation/historical notes).

steps.expected: No cfg gates
steps.observed: No #[cfg] gates found.

steps.expected: No matches
steps.observed: No matches. GIC is pub static GIC: GicV3 = GicV3 — concrete type, no vtable.

steps.expected: mrs ICC_IAR1_EL1, no nomem, nostack, spurious check at 1023
steps.observed: mrs s3_0_c12_c12_0 (ICC_IAR1_EL1), options(nostack) only — no nomem. Returns None for id == 1023 (SPURIOUS constant).

steps.expected: msr ICC_EOIR1_EL1, no nomem, nostack, DSB SY after
steps.observed: msr s3_0_c12_c12_1 (ICC_EOIR1_EL1), options(nostack) — no nomem. DSB SY barrier immediately after write.

assertions.title: Per-core CPU interface init via system registers

steps.expected: SRE=1 first, ISB after, then PMR/CTLR/IGRP1, no nomem
steps.observed: ICC_SRE_EL1 written FIRST with SRE=1 (orr bit 0). ISB immediately after. Then ICC_PMR_EL1 (0xFF), ICC_CTLR_EL1 (0, EOImode=0), ICC_IGRPEN1_EL1 (1). All use options(nostack) — no nomem on any ICC access.

steps.expected: gicr_base + core_id * 0x20000, wake sequence, SGI/PPI config
steps.observed: gicr_for_core(core_id) = GICR_BASE + core_id * GICR_STRIDE (0x20000). Clears ProcessorSleep, polls ChildrenAsleep. Sets IGROUPR0 to 0xFFFFFFFF, priorities to 0x80, enables SGI 0 + PPI 30 via GICR_ISENABLER0.

steps.expected: IROUTER used, ARE_NS set, DSB SY + ISB barriers
steps.observed: Disables GICD, sets SPI priorities via GICD_IPRIORITYR, routes via GICD_IROUTER (not ITARGETSR), enables with GICD_CTLR_ENABLE_GRP1_NS | GICD_CTLR_ARE_NS. DSB SY + ISB after final CTLR write.

assertions.title: DTB parsing for arm,gic-v3 with 2+ reg entries

steps.expected: arm,gic-v3 compat string, regs[0] and regs[1] extracted, handles 2+ entries
steps.observed: dt.find_first("arm,gic-v3") at line 638. Extracts regs[0] (GICD) and regs[1] (GICR). Guard: gic.regs.len() >= 2 — handles 2 or 3 entries gracefully. No cortex-a15-gic reference.

steps.action: Grep for gic-version= in all .sh files
steps.expected: Only gic-version=3, zero gic-version=2
steps.observed: 10 occurrences of gic-version=3 across all 6 scripts. Zero occurrences of gic-version=2.

steps.action: Read irq_handler and secondary_main in main.rs
steps.expected: Trait method calls on GIC instance
steps.observed: irq_handler: interrupt_controller::GIC.acknowledge() and GIC.end_of_interrupt(id). secondary_main: uses InterruptController trait import, calls GIC.init_per_core(core_id). All via trait methods.

steps.expected: GIC.enable_irq() via trait, not standalone function
steps.observed: interrupt_controller::GIC.enable_irq(IRQ_ID) at line 226. Import: use super::interrupt_controller::{self, InterruptController}. Uses trait method on GIC instance.

steps.expected: GIC.enable_irq()/disable_irq() via trait
steps.observed: All calls via interrupt_controller::GIC.enable_irq(irq) and GIC.disable_irq(irq). Import: use super::interrupt_controller::{self, InterruptController}.

steps.expected: msr icc_sgi1r_el1 with affinity encoding, no nomem
steps.observed: msr s3_0_c12_c11_5 (ICC_SGI1R_EL1). target_list = 1u64 << (target_core & 0xF) — SGI ID 0 (bits 27:24 = 0), Aff0 target list bit. options(nostack) — no nomem.

steps.expected: First invalid number is 28+, no 27

assertions.title: SAFETY comments on all unsafe blocks

steps.action: Count unsafe blocks and SAFETY comments in interrupt_controller.rs
steps.expected: SAFETY count >= unsafe count, zero nomem on ICC asm
steps.observed: 15 unsafe blocks, 15 SAFETY comments. Grep for 'options(.*nomem' in ICC code: NO MATCHES. All ICC asm uses options(nostack) without nomem.

steps.expected: Only in comment text explaining omission
steps.observed: 10 occurrences — all in comment text explaining 'nomem intentionally omitted'. Zero in actual asm options.

steps.expected: arm,gic-v3 present, no arm,cortex-a15-gic
steps.observed: strings output: 'arm,gic-v3' and 'arm,gic-v3-its'. No cortex-a15-gic.

steps.expected: All dumpdtb commands use gic-version=3
steps.observed: run-qemu.sh, smoke.sh, crash.sh, stress.sh all use gic-version=3 in dumpdtb commands.

steps.expected: is_idle: bool in PerCoreState, initialized to false
steps.observed: PerCoreState struct has 'is_idle: bool' (line 65). Initialized to false in const INIT (line 47): is_idle: false.

assertions.title: is_idle set when core runs idle thread

steps.action: Read schedule_inner idle fallback in scheduler.rs
steps.expected: is_idle = true on idle thread path
steps.observed: Line 410: s.cores[core].is_idle = true in the idle thread fallback branch of schedule_inner (when no runnable threads, idle thread is selected).

assertions.title: is_idle cleared when non-idle thread selected

steps.action: Read schedule_inner selection paths in scheduler.rs
steps.expected: is_idle = false on all non-idle selection paths
steps.observed: Line 380: s.cores[core].is_idle = false when selecting from run queue. Line 391: s.cores[core].is_idle = false when continuing with old thread (has budget). Both non-idle paths clear the flag.

summary: Tested 21 assertions: 21 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All GICv3 migration source inspection assertions verified. InterruptController trait defined with correct signatures, GicV3 implements all methods with real code, GICv2 artifacts fully removed from kernel source, static dispatch confirmed, ICC system register usage follows safety rules (no nomem), QEMU scripts all use gic-version=3, consumers migrated to trait methods, fuzz test updated, SAFETY comments present on all unsafe blocks, virt.dtb contains arm,gic-v3, and per-core idle tracking implemented correctly.
